expectations.text: Identifies the slow warmup opening as a retention killer
expectations.evidence: Problem 1 is explicitly titled 'Slow Warmup Opening — Kills the First 5 Seconds' with the analysis: 'There is zero confirmation of the thumbnail promise. Subconscious viewer reaction:
— and they're gone.' References the principle: 'The first 15 seconds must visually confirm what the thumbnail promised.' Clear, specific identification.

expectations.text: Flags the subscribe/bell ask in the first 30 seconds as a mistake
expectations.evidence: Problem 2 is explicitly 'Subscribe/Bell Ask in the First 30 Seconds' with analysis: 'This appears before the viewer has received any value from the video. The implicit message to the viewer is:
' The principle is stated: 'Anything that interrupts that to serve the channel's metrics triggers drop-off.'

expectations.text: Flags the sponsor read appearing before the premise is even established
expectations.evidence: Problem 3 is explicitly 'Sponsor Read Before the Premise Is Established' with analysis: 'The viewer has not yet confirmed this is the video they want to watch — they haven't seen the ball pit, they haven't heard the stakes, they have no investment in continuing. Placing a sponsor read here is asking someone to sit through an ad before they've agreed to watch the movie.'

expectations.text: Notes that the ball pit concept isn't visually shown until well into the script
expectations.evidence: Problem 4 explicitly addresses this: 'By the time the ball pit is even named verbally, we are already ~25–30 seconds into the video — and it has not been shown at all. The viewer clicked on a thumbnail showing a massive ball pit. They have been watching someone say hello, ask for a subscribe, and pitch VPN software.' Explicitly notes the visual absence.

expectations.text: Provides specific recommendations for how to rewrite the opening
expectations.evidence: A full rewrite is provided: '0–5 sec: Open ON the ball pit. Wide shot of an enormous sea of multicolored balls... 5–15 sec: Creator is already in the ball pit... 15–45 sec: Show scale. Quick cuts... 45–60 sec: One extreme visual — the creator dives fully under the surface...' Includes exact dialogue and visual direction.

claims.claim: All 5 problems identified map directly to SKILL.md common mistakes table
claims.evidence: Slow warmup, subscribe ask, sponsor placement, no visual confirmation, meandering premise — all appear in SKILL.md's Common Mistakes table

claims.claim: Summary table provided with timing and retention impact
claims.evidence: Response ends with a summary table: Mistake | Time It Occurs | Retention Impact — adds structure not requested but useful

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks whether the rewrite itself avoids the same mistakes it identified — a rewrite that still has filler warmup would pass 4/5 assertions. Consider: 'The rewrite does not contain filler warmup, subscribe ask, or sponsor in first 60 seconds.'

eval_feedback.overall: Strong eval. The 5 assertions map well to the 5 distinct problems in the input script. Minor gap: no assertion validates the quality of the rewrite itself.
